[Page 1]

When the +1 nucleosome gets in the way, TFIIH fails but does not fall François Robert1,2,3,* 1Institut de recherches cliniques de Montréal, 110 Avenue des Pins Ouest, Montréal, QC H2W 1R7, Canada 2Département de Médecine, Faculté de Médecine, Université de Montréal, 2900 Boul. Édouard-Montpetit, Montréal, QC H3T 1J4, Canada 3Faculty of Medicine, Division of Experimental Medicine, McGill University, Montréal, QC H3A 1A3, Canada

*Correspondence: francois.robert@ircm.qc.ca https://doi.org/10.1016/j.molcel.2023.05.003

In this issue of Molecular Cell, Abril-Garrido et al.1 used cryo-EM to uncover that the +1 nucleosome inhibits transcription by interfering with the function of the TFIIH translocase via mechanisms that depend on its position relative to the transcription start site. Eukaryotic DNA is packaged into nucleosomes, which have a significant impact on molecular processes occurring on DNA. Nucleosomes can impede the formation of the pre-initiation complex (PIC), composed of RNA polymerase II (RNAPII) and associated factors, on gene promoters. The +1 nucleosome flanking the nucleosome-free region where the PIC is assembled is intimately connected to early events in the transcription cycle, including PIC assembly, initiation, promoter escape, and promoter-proximal pausing, and released into elongation, though the exact mechanisms remain unclear. Abril-Garrido and colleagues used cryoelectron microscopy (cryo-EM) to investigate the effects of the +1 nucleosome’s position on PIC assembly.1 The authors found that in cells, as the +1 nucleosome moves closer to the transcription start site (TSS), the associated gene’s transcription decreases. When the distance between the edge of the +1 nucleosome and the TSS is 40 base pairs (bp), genes are often highly transcribed. However, when this distance is reduced to 20 bp or 10 bp, transcription levels tend to be reduced. The researchers then reproduced this relationship in an in vitro transcription assay and determined the corresponding PIC structures by high-resolution cryo-EM. When a nucleosome was positioned over the TSS, PIC assembly did not occur (Figure 1, PIC-Nuc0W), consistent with pioneer work from more than 35 years ago.2–4 If the +1 nucleosome was positioned so that one turn of DNA (10 bp) separates its edge from the TSS, a PIC did assemble with all components, but these

PICs were transcriptionally ineffective (Figure 1, PIC-Nuc10W). The PIC-Nuc10W structure is very similar to PICs assembled on naked DNA,5 except for one key component: transcription factor IIH (TFIIH). TFIIH is unique among the so-called general transcription factors (GTFs) in that it carries enzymatic activities. It notably contains a kinase (CDK7) that phosphorylates the C-terminal domain of RNAPII, and a translocase (xeroderma pigmentosum group B, XPB) that is critical for opening the DNA duplex, a step required for localizing the template strand in the RNAPII active site. The XPB translocase contains two lobes that grab DNA downstream of the polymerase and translocate that DNA toward the polymerase. Forcing the DNA inside the polymerase promotes the two DNA strands to locally unwind. In the PICNuc10W structure, the XPB translocase does not have sufficient DNA to grab onto the DNA between the TSS and the nucleosome. Instead, XPB lobe 2 contacts the DNA on the nucleosome surface, while lobe 1 is 30 Å away from the DNA. In addition, XPB contacts xeroderma pigmentosum group D (XPD), another TFIIH subunit, forming a "closed" conformation that was previously observed in TFIIH when not associated with DNA.6 Thus, while 10 bp between the TSS and the +1 nucleosome allows PIC assembly, there is not enough free DNA for TFIIH to position itself in a productive conformation. Increasing the TSS-nucleosome distance by eight additional bp enabled TFIIH to properly position itself within the PIC and make proper contacts with downstream DNA (Figure 1, PIC-Nuc18W). For that to happen, about 20 bp of nucleosomal DNA

are unwrapped from the nucleosome. TFIIH therefore forces itself in, probably aided by the natural "breathing" of nucleosomal DNA. The PIC-Nuc18W was slightly more transcriptionally active than PICNuc10W but was still impaired. Although the XPB translocase can grasp the DNA, it is limited in its ability to translocate DNA toward the polymerase. To achieve this, it would need to extract DNA from the +1 nucleosome, which is not easily done beyond the first two superhelical turns already unwrapped. Consequently, when ribonucleotides were added, TFIIH was unable to translocate more than 2 bp of DNA toward the polymerase, which was not enough for promoter opening. Thus, a 20 bp TSS-nucleosome distance allows proper TFIIH association but does not leave enough DNA for translocation to occur without further nucleosome remodeling. In a previous study, Chen et al.7 determined the structure of PICs in the presence of nucleosomes positioned further downstream of the TSS at 40, 50, and 70 bp.7 Typically, genes with such +1 nucleosome positions are expressed at higher levels in vivo. Interestingly, these nucleosomes did not impede TFIIH activity and could even stimulate transcription when located 40 bp after the TSS (Figure 1, PICNuc40W). Chen et al.7 proposed that this nucleosome stabilizes the PIC as they observed interactions between the histones and nucleosomal DNA with both TFIIH and the PIC-associated Mediator. Such positive effects of the +1 nucleosome on PIC assembly were previously proposed to occur via interactions between TFIID and acetylated8 and methylated9 histone tails. Interestingly, in this system, the

Molecular Cell 83, June 1, 2023 a 2023 Elsevier Inc. 1763

[Page 2]

nucleosome is an unanswered question that deserves investigation. To conclude, the +1 nucleosome is located near the area where RNAPII pauses. How the position of the +1 nucleosome impacts pausing and whether pausing is used to ‘‘prepare’’ the polymerase for negotiating the +1 nucleosome are open questions. 5. Farnung, L., and Vos, S.M. (2022). Assembly of RNA polymerase II transcription initiation complexes. Curr. Opin. Struct. Biol. 73, 102335.

stimulatory effect of the +1 nucleosome was not observed when the nucleosome was positioned further away. Therefore, the +1 nucleosome can have neutral (>50 bp), positive (40 bp), or negative (<20 bp) effects on initiation depending on its location (see Figure 1). Moving forward, it will be important to investigate the impact of the +1 nucleosome on transcription in the presence of co-activators, such as TFIID and Mediator. These co-activators stabilize the PIC, and Mediator plays a crucial role in positioning the TFIIH kinase (CDK7) near its substrate, the RNAPII C-terminal domain.10 In the PIC structures with the nucleosome positioned at 40 bp after the TSS (which included TFIID and Mediator),

6. Greber, B.J., Toso, D.B., Fang, J., and Nogales, E. (2019). The complete structure of the human TFIIH core complex. Elife 8, e44771.

1764 Molecular Cell 83, June 1, 2023

Mediator was observed to contact the nucleosome, potentially contributing to the positive effect on initiation mentioned above.7 It remains an open question whether moving the nucleosome closer to the TSS would impede TFIID and/or Mediator assembly and/or function, similar to the effect observed on TFIIH. Aside from its effect on promoter opening and initiation, the +1 nucleosome, like downstream nucleosomes, presents an obstacle to elongation. However, transcription through the +1 nucleosome is achieved by RNAPII that has not yet "matured" into an elongation complex associated with elongation factors and histone chaperones.
